edibility: Medicinal — used in traditional Chinese medicine (TCM), taken as tea, extract, or capsule; cultivated on grain substrate; cordycepin compound under research for potential therapeutic applications.
taste: Earthy, mildly bitter, with faint fungal sweetness.
search: Found in late summer and fall, parasitic on buried lepidopteran pupae in grasslands and woodland litter. Identified by distinctive orange club-shaped fruiting body. Look-alikes include Tolypocladium species.
